name: Normal-mode custom benchmark with native auto linear models

description: Ran custom synthetic benchmark in normal mode with AutoDLinear/AutoNLinear/AutoTimeBase/AutoTimeBaseTrend. Benchmark crashed after 19.3s because Ray Tune reported 'No best trial found for the given metric: loss' for an auto linear trial, indicating no valid loss was reported under the current normal preset/workload.

Diagnostic run isolated the normal-mode crash source without code changes. AutoDLinear, AutoNLinear, and AutoTimeBase completed, while AutoTimeBaseTrend alone failed with 'No best trial found for the given metric: loss'. This indicates the benchmark crash is caused by AutoTimeBaseTrend under the current normal preset on the synthetic monthly dataset.

Re-ran the provided custom benchmark script in normal mode on logs/smoke_benchmarks/synthetic_monthly.csv. It crashed again after 19.5s with the same Ray Tune 'No best trial found for the given metric: loss' failure, so normal-mode benchmark results are still unavailable.
Red test run after rewriting benchmark unit tests exposed a pre-existing IndentationError in devtools/benchmark_long_horizon.py collection path.
Reverted benchmark coverage to default DLinear/NLinear/TimeBase/TimeBaseTrend plus SeasonalNaive and MFLES; removed auto-search CLI expectations and verified the updated unit tests pass.
Quality gate: make format passed; no benchmark logic change measured. Keeping working tree for further validation.
